reply_content:
We don’t know whether Wissa was 100% fit, which might be why Howe didn’t bring him on earlier. And Woltemade obviously can’t be expected to play full minutes either, with the upcoming congested schedule of 13 matches in 43 days. 

But you’re absolutely right that it’s screaming out for a Plan B when we can play a 4-4-3 with 75% possession and still not really look dangerous.

Instead of a 4-4-2, I’d much prefer a 4-2-3-1. Both Ramsey and Elanga played their best football for Villa and Forest respectively in that system. They could flank Woltemade through the middle, with Wissa up top. I’ve never understood the logic of spending £40m and £55m on players, only to play them out of position rather than where they actually excelled and where they earned their moves.

That said, for this system to work, strengthening the attacking options on the bench is a must first. Right now we have no backups if one of our two “Ws” breaks down or gets run into the ground during a match. And that’s undoubtedly also why Howe isn’t playing them together at the moment. The risk of one of them getting injured is unbearable, because then the other would have to play 13 full matches in six weeks.

No one knows why Osula’s return keeps being delayed. And there’s hardly much appetite to throw another £55m at Jørgen Strand Larsen.
